presentation.title: AI Workbench Local — Policy / Contract Comparison
presentation.subtitle: Executive comparison review of grounded document differences
presentation.author: AI Workbench Local

executive_summary:
Executive Comparison:  
The Access Review Evidence Log (EVD-101) is a formal register tracking evidence completion for Q1 2024 vendor control reviews, requiring documented proof such as user exports and approval emails to close controls, while the Privileged Account Approval Email grants limited, time-bound privileged access to two support administrators under strict usage rules and mandatory quarterly vendor revalidation.

- EVD-101 mandates archiving of specific artifacts (e.g., email-export) in an approved repository to satisfy evidence requirements.  
- The Privileged Account Approval Email explicitly requires quarterly vendor confirmation of personnel for ongoing access.  
- EVD-101 focuses on evidence completeness for control closure; the email governs active access permissions and usage constraints.  
- Evidence gaps in EVD-101 block control closure until verified; the email’s access is inherently time-limited and subject to revalidation.  
- Both documents tie to vendor control processes but operate at different stages: evidence tracking vs. access authorization.  
- The email’s scope is narrowly defined to two administrators and specific tasks, whereas EVD-101 applies broadly to all evidence items in the review.
key_highlights: The Access Review Evidence Log (EVD-101) is a formal register tracking evidence completion for Q1 2024 vendor control reviews, requiring documented proof such as user exports and approval emails to close controls, while the Privileged Account Approval Email grants limited, time-bound privileged access to two support administrators under strict usage rules and mandatory quarterly vendor revalidation.
EVD-101 mandates archiving of specific artifacts (e.g., email-export) in an approved repository to satisfy evidence requirements.
The Privileged Account Approval Email explicitly requires quarterly vendor confirmation of personnel for ongoing access.
EVD-101 focuses on evidence completeness for control closure; the email governs active access permissions and usage constraints.
Evidence gaps in EVD-101 block control closure until verified; the email’s access is inherently time-limited and subject to revalidation.
Both documents tie to vendor control processes but operate at different stages: evidence tracking vs. access authorization.
